00029 
00030 #include <types.h>
00031 #include <kern/errno.h>
00032 #include <lib.h>
00033 #include <spl.h>
00034 #include <spinlock.h>
00035 #include <thread.h>
00036 #include <current.h>
00037 #include <mips/tlb.h>
00038 #include <addrspace.h>
00039 #include <vm.h>
00040 
00041 /*
00042  * Dumb MIPS-only "VM system" that is intended to only be just barely
00043  * enough to struggle off the ground. You should replace all of this
00044  * code while doing the VM assignment. In fact, starting in that
00045  * assignment, this file is not included in your kernel!
00046  */
00047 
00048 /* under dumbvm, always have 48k of user stack */
00049 #define DUMBVM_STACKPAGES    12
00050 
00051 /*
00052  * Wrap rma_stealmem in a spinlock.
00053  */
00054 static struct spinlock stealmem_lock = SPINLOCK_INITIALIZER;
00055 
00056 void
00057 vm_bootstrap(void)
00058 {
00059         /* Do nothing. */
00060 }
00061 
00062 static
00063 paddr_t
00064 getppages(unsigned long npages)
00065 {
00066         paddr_t addr;
00067 
00068         spinlock_acquire(&stealmem_lock);
00069 
00070         addr = ram_stealmem(npages);
00071         
00072         spinlock_release(&stealmem_lock);
00073         return addr;
00074 }
00075 
00076 /* Allocate/free some kernel-space virtual pages */
00077 vaddr_t 
00078 alloc_kpages(int npages)
00079 {
00080         paddr_t pa;
00081         pa = getppages(npages);
00082         if (pa==0) {
00083                 return 0;
00084         }
00085         return PADDR_TO_KVADDR(pa);
00086 }
00087 
00088 void 
00089 free_kpages(vaddr_t addr)
00090 {
00091         /* nothing - leak the memory. */
00092 
00093         (void)addr;
00094 }
00095 
00096 void
00097 vm_tlbshootdown_all(void)
00098 {
00099         panic("dumbvm tried to do tlb shootdown?!\n");
00100 }
00101 
00102 void
00103 vm_tlbshootdown(const struct tlbshootdown *ts)
00104 {
00105         (void)ts;
00106         panic("dumbvm tried to do tlb shootdown?!\n");
00107 }
00108 
00109 int
00110 vm_fault(int faulttype, vaddr_t faultaddress)
00111 {
00112         vaddr_t vbase1, vtop1, vbase2, vtop2, stackbase, stacktop;
00113         paddr_t paddr;
00114         int i;
00115         uint32_t ehi, elo;
00116         struct addrspace *as;
00117         int spl;
00118 
00119         faultaddress &= PAGE_FRAME;
00120 
00121         DEBUG(DB_VM, "dumbvm: fault: 0x%x\n", faultaddress);
00122 
00123         switch (faulttype) {
00124             case VM_FAULT_READONLY:
00125                 /* We always create pages read-write, so we can't get this */
00126                 panic("dumbvm: got VM_FAULT_READONLY\n");
00127             case VM_FAULT_READ:
00128             case VM_FAULT_WRITE:
00129                 break;
00130             default:
00131                 return EINVAL;
00132         }
00133 
00134         as = curthread->t_addrspace;
00135         if (as == NULL) {
00136                 /*
00137                  * No address space set up. This is probably a kernel
00138                  * fault early in boot. Return EFAULT so as to panic
00139                  * instead of getting into an infinite faulting loop.
00140                  */
00141                 return EFAULT;
00142         }
00143 
00144         /* Assert that the address space has been set up properly. */
00145         KASSERT(as->as_vbase1 != 0);
00146         KASSERT(as->as_pbase1 != 0);
00147         KASSERT(as->as_npages1 != 0);
00148         KASSERT(as->as_vbase2 != 0);
00149         KASSERT(as->as_pbase2 != 0);
00150         KASSERT(as->as_npages2 != 0);
00151         KASSERT(as->as_stackpbase != 0);
00152         KASSERT((as->as_vbase1 & PAGE_FRAME) == as->as_vbase1);
00153         KASSERT((as->as_pbase1 & PAGE_FRAME) == as->as_pbase1);
00154         KASSERT((as->as_vbase2 & PAGE_FRAME) == as->as_vbase2);
00155         KASSERT((as->as_pbase2 & PAGE_FRAME) == as->as_pbase2);
00156         KASSERT((as->as_stackpbase & PAGE_FRAME) == as->as_stackpbase);
00157 
00158         vbase1 = as->as_vbase1;
00159         vtop1 = vbase1 + as->as_npages1 * PAGE_SIZE;
00160         vbase2 = as->as_vbase2;
00161         vtop2 = vbase2 + as->as_npages2 * PAGE_SIZE;
00162         stackbase = USERSTACK - DUMBVM_STACKPAGES * PAGE_SIZE;
00163         stacktop = USERSTACK;
00164 
00165         if (faultaddress >= vbase1 && faultaddress < vtop1) {
00166                 paddr = (faultaddress - vbase1) + as->as_pbase1;
00167         }
00168         else if (faultaddress >= vbase2 && faultaddress < vtop2) {
00169                 paddr = (faultaddress - vbase2) + as->as_pbase2;
00170         }
00171         else if (faultaddress >= stackbase && faultaddress < stacktop) {
00172                 paddr = (faultaddress - stackbase) + as->as_stackpbase;
00173         }
00174         else {
00175                 return EFAULT;
00176         }
00177 
00178         /* make sure it's page-aligned */
00179         KASSERT((paddr & PAGE_FRAME) == paddr);
00180 
00181         /* Disable interrupts on this CPU while frobbing the TLB. */
00182         spl = splhigh();
00183 
00184         for (i=0; i<NUM_TLB; i++) {
00185                 tlb_read(&ehi, &elo, i);
00186                 if (elo & TLBLO_VALID) {
00187                         continue;
00188                 }
00189                 ehi = faultaddress;
00190                 elo = paddr | TLBLO_DIRTY | TLBLO_VALID;
00191                 DEBUG(DB_VM, "dumbvm: 0x%x -> 0x%x\n", faultaddress, paddr);
00192                 tlb_write(ehi, elo, i);
00193                 splx(spl);
00194                 return 0;
00195         }
00196 
00197         kprintf("dumbvm: Ran out of TLB entries - cannot handle page fault\n");
00198         splx(spl);
00199         return EFAULT;
00200 }
00201 
00202 struct addrspace *
00203 as_create(void)
00204 {
00205         struct addrspace *as = kmalloc(sizeof(struct addrspace));
00206         if (as==NULL) {
00207                 return NULL;
00208         }
00209 
00210         as->as_vbase1 = 0;
00211         as->as_pbase1 = 0;
00212         as->as_npages1 = 0;
00213         as->as_vbase2 = 0;
00214         as->as_pbase2 = 0;
00215         as->as_npages2 = 0;
00216         as->as_stackpbase = 0;
00217 
00218         return as;
00219 }
00220 
00221 void
00222 as_destroy(struct addrspace *as)
00223 {
00224         kfree(as);
00225 }
00226 
00227 void
00228 as_activate(struct addrspace *as)
00229 {
00230         int i, spl;
00231 
00232         (void)as;
00233 
00234         /* Disable interrupts on this CPU while frobbing the TLB. */
00235         spl = splhigh();
00236 
00237         for (i=0; i<NUM_TLB; i++) {
00238                 tlb_write(TLBHI_INVALID(i), TLBLO_INVALID(), i);
00239         }
00240 
00241         splx(spl);
00242 }
00243 
00244 int
00245 as_define_region(struct addrspace *as, vaddr_t vaddr, size_t sz,
00246                  int readable, int writeable, int executable)
00247 {
00248         size_t npages; 
00249 
00250         /* Align the region. First, the base... */
00251         sz += vaddr & ~(vaddr_t)PAGE_FRAME;
00252         vaddr &= PAGE_FRAME;
00253 
00254         /* ...and now the length. */
00255         sz = (sz + PAGE_SIZE - 1) & PAGE_FRAME;
00256 
00257         npages = sz / PAGE_SIZE;
00258 
00259         /* We don't use these - all pages are read-write */
00260         (void)readable;
00261         (void)writeable;
00262         (void)executable;
00263 
00264         if (as->as_vbase1 == 0) {
00265                 as->as_vbase1 = vaddr;
00266                 as->as_npages1 = npages;
00267                 return 0;
00268         }
00269 
00270         if (as->as_vbase2 == 0) {
00271                 as->as_vbase2 = vaddr;
00272                 as->as_npages2 = npages;
00273                 return 0;
00274         }
00275 
00276         /*
00277          * Support for more than two regions is not available.
00278          */
00279         kprintf("dumbvm: Warning: too many regions\n");
00280         return EUNIMP;
00281 }
00282 
00283 static
00284 void
00285 as_zero_region(paddr_t paddr, unsigned npages)
00286 {
00287         bzero((void *)PADDR_TO_KVADDR(paddr), npages * PAGE_SIZE);
00288 }
00289 
00290 int
00291 as_prepare_load(struct addrspace *as)
00292 {
00293         KASSERT(as->as_pbase1 == 0);
00294         KASSERT(as->as_pbase2 == 0);
00295         KASSERT(as->as_stackpbase == 0);
00296 
00297         as->as_pbase1 = getppages(as->as_npages1);
00298         if (as->as_pbase1 == 0) {
00299                 return ENOMEM;
00300         }
00301 
00302         as->as_pbase2 = getppages(as->as_npages2);
00303         if (as->as_pbase2 == 0) {
00304                 return ENOMEM;
00305         }
00306 
00307         as->as_stackpbase = getppages(DUMBVM_STACKPAGES);
00308         if (as->as_stackpbase == 0) {
00309                 return ENOMEM;
00310         }
00311         
00312         as_zero_region(as->as_pbase1, as->as_npages1);
00313         as_zero_region(as->as_pbase2, as->as_npages2);
00314         as_zero_region(as->as_stackpbase, DUMBVM_STACKPAGES);
00315 
00316         return 0;
00317 }
00318 
00319 int
00320 as_complete_load(struct addrspace *as)
00321 {
00322         (void)as;
00323         return 0;
00324 }
00325 
00326 int
00327 as_define_stack(struct addrspace *as, vaddr_t *stackptr)
00328 {
00329         KASSERT(as->as_stackpbase != 0);
00330 
00331         *stackptr = USERSTACK;
00332         return 0;
00333 }
00334 
00335 int
00336 as_copy(struct addrspace *old, struct addrspace **ret)
00337 {
00338         struct addrspace *new;
00339 
00340         new = as_create();
00341         if (new==NULL) {
00342                 return ENOMEM;
00343         }
00344 
00345         new->as_vbase1 = old->as_vbase1;
00346         new->as_npages1 = old->as_npages1;
00347         new->as_vbase2 = old->as_vbase2;
00348         new->as_npages2 = old->as_npages2;
00349 
00350         /* (Mis)use as_prepare_load to allocate some physical memory. */
00351         if (as_prepare_load(new)) {
00352                 as_destroy(new);
00353                 return ENOMEM;
00354         }
00355 
00356         KASSERT(new->as_pbase1 != 0);
00357         KASSERT(new->as_pbase2 != 0);
00358         KASSERT(new->as_stackpbase != 0);
00359 
00360         memmove((void *)PADDR_TO_KVADDR(new->as_pbase1),
00361                 (const void *)PADDR_TO_KVADDR(old->as_pbase1),
00362                 old->as_npages1*PAGE_SIZE);
00363 
00364         memmove((void *)PADDR_TO_KVADDR(new->as_pbase2),
00365                 (const void *)PADDR_TO_KVADDR(old->as_pbase2),
00366                 old->as_npages2*PAGE_SIZE);
00367 
00368         memmove((void *)PADDR_TO_KVADDR(new->as_stackpbase),
00369                 (const void *)PADDR_TO_KVADDR(old->as_stackpbase),
00370                 DUMBVM_STACKPAGES*PAGE_SIZE);
00371         
00372         *ret = new;
00373         return 0;
00374 }
Generated on Sun Jun 27 22:12:13 2010 for Os/161 by  1.6.1
